body:
Title: Revenu Quebec Schedule H 2025 tax credit for caregivers.

Source:
- https://www.revenuquebec.ca/en/online-services/forms-and-publications/current-details/tp-1-v/
- https://www.revenuquebec.ca/documents/en/formulaires/tp/2025-12/TP-1.D.H-V%282025-12%29.pdf

Schedule H numeric source values for encoded amounts, rates, thresholds, caps, and limits:
Value: 18
Value: 70
Value: 365
Value: 183
Value: 90
Value: 26520
Value: 16
Value: 0.16
Value: 2988
Value: 1494
Value: 5200
Value: 30
Value: 0.30
Value: 1560
Value: 12

Schedule H Part B section 1 calculates the caregiver credit for a person 18 or over with an impairment who needs assistance and lived with the caregiver. Lines 252 through 254 subtract $26,520 from the care receiver's net income and floor the result at zero. Line 256 multiplies the excess by 16%. Line 251 gives a base amount of $2,988. Line 257 uses the lesser of line 256 and $1,494. Line 258 subtracts line 257 from line 251. Lines 259 and 260 enter the reduction for a care receiver who turned 18 in the year and an adjustment of social assistance payments for a handicapped child 18 or over attending secondary general education. Line 261 adds lines 259 and 260. Line 262 subtracts line 261 from line 258. Line 263 subtracts the amount claimed by another caregiver. Line 265 adds the resulting line 264 amounts. Lines 266 and 267 calculate the additional amount for specialized respite services by applying 30% to eligible expenses capped at $5,200 per eligible care receiver, for a maximum additional amount of $1,560 per person. Line 268 adds line 265 and line 267 and carries the result to TP-1 line 462.

Schedule H Part B section 2 calculates the caregiver credit for a person 18 or over with an impairment who needs assistance and did not live with the caregiver. Lines 352 through 354 subtract $26,520 from the care receiver's net income and floor the result at zero. Line 356 multiplies the excess by 16%. Line 351 gives a base amount of $1,494. Line 357 uses the lesser of line 356 and $1,494. Line 358 subtracts line 357 from line 351. Lines 359 and 360 are the turned-18 reduction and social assistance adjustment. Line 361 adds lines 359 and 360. Line 362 subtracts line 361 from line 358. Line 363 subtracts the amount claimed by another caregiver. Line 365 adds the resulting line 364 amounts and carries the result to TP-1 line 462.

Schedule H Part C calculates the caregiver credit for a person 70 or over who lived with the caregiver and is not the caregiver's spouse. Line 451 gives a base amount of $1,494. Line 452 subtracts the amount claimed by another caregiver. Line 454 adds the resulting line 453 amounts and carries the result to TP-1 line 462. The work chart for a care receiver who turned 18 divides the amount from line 258 or 358 by 12 and multiplies by the number of months preceding the care receiver's birthday, including the month of the birthday. Schedule H conditions include Quebec residence on December 31, 2025; a care or co-residence period of at least 365 consecutive days including at least 183 days in 2025; and, for split credits, at least 90 days in 2025 for each caregiver.
